data.description:
## Description
Foundation seam for the code_health lens. Introduce a language-agnostic analyzer protocol and registry that the metric readers call instead of scanning hardcoded paths. No tool shell-outs yet — ship the protocol, the registry, and a null/default analyzer so the contract is testable in isolation (additive; changes no existing behavior).

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `analyze(repo_root, languages) -> AnalyzerResult` protocol defined (fields: loc, complexity, duplication — each optional/None when unsupported).
- [ ] An analyzer *registry* keyed by language (or a single configured analyzer) with a lookup that returns None when no analyzer is configured for a language.
- [ ] HARD fallback contract encoded as a helper: no analyzer configured for <lang> -> returns `Unavailable("no analyzer configured for <lang>")`; NEVER scans a path, NEVER fabricates zeros, NEVER leaks an exception string as the reason.
- [ ] Pure stdlib; importing it registers nothing into REGISTRY as a side effect (opt-in wiring lands later).

## Verification
- [ ] Unit tests: lookup-miss -> Unavailable with the exact reason; supported -> AnalyzerResult passthrough.
- [ ] `make lint` + targeted `pytest tests/unit/metrics`.

## Dependencies: None (keystone)
## Files likely touched: src/rebar/metrics/analyzer.py (new), tests/unit/metrics/test_analyzer.py (new)
## Estimated scope: M (2-3 files)
data.title: Define the code-health analyzer interface + hard Unavailable fallback contract
